task: Write an SEO blog post about how AI agents can replace a DevRel team
kb_context: OpenClaw is a self-hosted AI assistant you run on your own devices. Multi-channel, multi-model, multi-agent with cron automation, skills system, and plugin architecture. Available at openclaw.ai.
competitive_context: Manual DevRel teams cost $500K-$1M/year. Tools like Orbit and Common Room provide analytics but not execution.

task: Write landing page copy for OpenClaw
kb_context: 10 agents replace a full DevRel + Sales team. Hub-and-spoke architecture with Atlas orchestrator. Weekly automated cycle: triage Monday, synthesize Tuesday, experiment Wednesday, create Thursday, report Friday.
competitive_context: No direct competitor offers end-to-end automated DevRel. Closest alternatives are point solutions: Orbit for community, Buffer for social, Mailchimp for email.

task: Create a batch of 5 Twitter/X posts promoting OpenClaw
kb_context: 10 AI agents that automate DevRel. Open-source. Works with any DevTools product. Agents: Sage (community), Echo (social), Iris (feedback), Nova (growth), Kai (content), Vox (video), Dex (docs), Rex (competitive), Pax (sales), Mox (campaigns).
competitive_context: DevRel teams are expensive and hard to scale. Most companies can't afford 10 specialists.

task: Write a press release announcing OpenClaw v1.0 launch
kb_context: First open-source system of 10 specialized AI agents that replaces a full DevRel + Sales team. Uses Claude for LLM, MCP for tool protocol. Demonstrated on PostHog (open-source product analytics).
competitive_context: No existing product automates the full DevRel lifecycle. This is a new category.

task: Create a product launch campaign brief for OpenClaw targeting DevTools founders
kb_context: Target audience: DevTools companies with 20-200 employees who can't afford a full DevRel team. Value prop: 10 agents for the price of one contractor. Weekly automated DevRel cycle.
competitive_context: DevTools companies typically spend 30-40% of budget on developer engagement. Most rely on founders doing DevRel part-time.
